mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightness, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day returns, with a directional accuracy rate below 52% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle phases reflects sustained capacity utilization and pricing power, which historically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, resulting in a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat in more than 60% of instances following the inflation threshold breach, invalidating the discount rate compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth exceeding 50,000 jobs combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market healing, which historically precedes earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a directional accuracy below 50% in predicting 20-day returns during the specified regime, indicating no predictive edge.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized sustains corporate earnings momentum, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates above 2.0% consistently coincide with negative or flat 60-day equity returns, breaking the earnings-momentum linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1.5 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which historically drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases following the housing threshold breach, refuting the demand contraction channel.
